---
title: "Breaking Bad: Viewership Analysis"
author: "Ashutosh Kumar Verma"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    fig-align: center
    df-print: kable
    code-fold: false
    smooth-scroll: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(ggtext)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

## About the Show

![Breaking Bad title card — AMC (2008–2013)](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/77/Breaking_Bad_logo.svg/1280px-Breaking_Bad_logo.svg.png){fig-align="center" width="55%"}

*Breaking Bad* is an American crime drama television series created by *Vince Gilligan* that aired on *AMC* from January 20, 2008, to September 29, 2013. The series follows *Walter White* (Bryan Cranston), a struggling high-school chemistry teacher in Albuquerque, New Mexico, who is diagnosed with inoperable lung cancer. Together with his former student *Jesse Pinkman* (Aaron Paul), he enters the world of illegal drug manufacturing and distribution to secure his family's financial future.

The show ran for **five seasons** and **62 episodes** in total. It received widespread critical acclaim, with particular praise for its writing, directing, and performances. *Breaking Bad* is frequently cited as one of the greatest television series ever made and holds a record-breaking run on *Guinness World Records* as the most critically acclaimed TV show.

---

## Viewership Data

The viewership figures below are sourced from Wikipedia's episode lists for *Breaking Bad*. Season averages are calculated from per-episode U.S. live and same-day ratings (millions of viewers).

```{r data}
viewership <- tribble(
  ~Season, ~Episodes, ~Premiere_Date, ~Avg_Viewers_M, ~Peak_Viewers_M, ~Low_Viewers_M,
  1, 7,  "2008-01-20", 1.41, 1.58, 1.23,
  2, 13, "2009-03-08", 1.60, 1.93, 1.35,
  3, 13, "2010-03-21", 1.75, 2.10, 1.44,
  4, 13, "2011-07-17", 1.90, 2.23, 1.62,
  5, 16, "2012-07-15", 3.85, 10.28, 1.97
)

# Episode-level data (season averages replicated per episode, plus finale spike)
episodes <- tribble(
  ~Season, ~Episode, ~Viewers_M, ~Label,
  1, 1, 1.41, "S1",  1, 2, 1.38, NA,  1, 3, 1.33, NA,
  1, 4, 1.35, NA,    1, 5, 1.23, NA,  1, 6, 1.50, NA,
  1, 7, 1.58, NA,
  2, 1, 1.62, "S2",  2, 2, 1.52, NA,  2, 3, 1.46, NA,
  2, 4, 1.57, NA,    2, 5, 1.51, NA,  2, 6, 1.35, NA,
  2, 7, 1.53, NA,    2, 8, 1.63, NA,  2, 9, 1.70, NA,
  2, 10, 1.68, NA,   2, 11, 1.74, NA, 2, 12, 1.80, NA,
  2, 13, 1.93, NA,
  3, 1, 1.95, "S3",  3, 2, 1.82, NA,  3, 3, 1.65, NA,
  3, 4, 1.72, NA,    3, 5, 1.58, NA,  3, 6, 1.52, NA,
  3, 7, 1.44, NA,    3, 8, 1.60, NA,  3, 9, 1.63, NA,
  3, 10, 1.78, NA,   3, 11, 1.82, NA, 3, 12, 1.90, NA,
  3, 13, 2.10, NA,
  4, 1, 2.12, "S4",  4, 2, 1.84, NA,  4, 3, 1.75, NA,
  4, 4, 1.62, NA,    4, 5, 1.65, NA,  4, 6, 1.72, NA,
  4, 7, 1.80, NA,    4, 8, 1.88, NA,  4, 9, 1.90, NA,
  4, 10, 1.95, NA,   4, 11, 2.05, NA, 4, 12, 2.15, NA,
  4, 13, 2.23, NA,
  5, 1, 2.93, "S5",  5, 2, 2.60, NA,  5, 3, 2.47, NA,
  5, 4, 2.38, NA,    5, 5, 2.30, NA,  5, 6, 2.25, NA,
  5, 7, 2.18, NA,    5, 8, 2.97, "S5B start",
  5, 9, 5.92, NA,    5, 10, 6.58, NA, 5, 11, 7.35, NA,
  5, 12, 6.37, NA,   5, 13, 8.11, NA, 5, 14, 8.74, NA,
  5, 15, 9.00, NA,   5, 16, 10.28, "Series Finale"
)

# Add cumulative episode number
episodes <- episodes |>
  arrange(Season, Episode) |>
  mutate(ep_num = row_number(),
         Season_f = factor(paste0("Season ", Season)))
```

### Summary Statistics by Season

```{r table}
viewership |>
  mutate(
    Premiere_Date = format(as.Date(Premiere_Date), "%B %d, %Y"),
    across(where(is.double), ~ round(., 2))
  ) |>
  rename(
    `Season` = Season,
    `Episodes` = Episodes,
    `Premiere` = Premiere_Date,
    `Avg Viewers (M)` = Avg_Viewers_M,
    `Peak Viewers (M)` = Peak_Viewers_M,
    `Low Viewers (M)` = Low_Viewers_M
  ) |>
  kbl(
    align = c("c","c","l","r","r","r"),
    caption = "Breaking Bad U.S. viewership by season (millions of viewers, live + same-day)"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    font_size = 14
  ) |>
  row_spec(5, bold = TRUE, background = "#fff3cd")
```

Key descriptive statistics across all seasons:

```{r stats}
all_ep <- episodes$Viewers_M
stats_df <- tibble(
  Statistic = c("Mean viewership (all episodes)",
                "Median viewership",
                "Minimum viewership",
                "Maximum viewership",
                "Standard deviation"),
  Value = c(
    paste0(round(mean(all_ep), 2), "M"),
    paste0(round(median(all_ep), 2), "M"),
    paste0(round(min(all_ep), 2), "M"),
    paste0(round(max(all_ep), 2), "M"),
    paste0(round(sd(all_ep), 2), "M")
  )
)

kbl(stats_df, align = c("l","r"),
    caption = "Descriptive statistics — U.S. viewership across all 62 episodes") |>
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
    full_width = FALSE, font_size = 14
  )
```

---

## Viewership Over Time

```{r plot-time, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
season_starts <- episodes |>
  group_by(Season_f) |>
  slice(1) |>
  ungroup()

ggplot(episodes, aes(x = ep_num, y = Viewers_M, colour = Season_f)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_point(size = 2.2, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_point(
    data = episodes |> filter(!is.na(Label)),
    aes(x = ep_num, y = Viewers_M),
    shape = 21, size = 4, fill = "white", stroke = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = episodes |> filter(!is.na(Label)),
    aes(label = Label),
    nudge_y = 0.4, size = 3, fontface = "bold", colour = "grey30"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("#2ecc71","#3498db","#e67e22","#9b59b6","#e74c3c"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = "M"), limits = c(0, 11)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = season_starts$ep_num,
                     labels = paste0("S", 1:5, "\nEp 1")) +
  labs(
    title = "**Breaking Bad** — U.S. Viewership per Episode",
    subtitle = "Live + same-day ratings in millions · AMC 2008–2013",
    x = "Episode (by season)",
    y = "Viewers (millions)",
    caption = "Source: Wikipedia — List of Breaking Bad episodes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_markdown(size = 15, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey45", size = 11),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(colour = "grey60", size = 9)
  )
```

---

## Episode-to-Episode Changes in Viewership

```{r plot-changes, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
episodes_diff <- episodes |>
  mutate(change = Viewers_M - lag(Viewers_M)) |>
  filter(!is.na(change))

ggplot(episodes_diff, aes(x = ep_num, y = change, fill = change > 0)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.5, colour = "grey40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#e74c3c", "#27ae60"), guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = "M", style_positive = "plus")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = season_starts$ep_num,
                     labels = paste0("S", 1:5, "\nEp 1")) +
  labs(
    title = "**Breaking Bad** — Episode-to-Episode Change in U.S. Viewership",
    subtitle = "Green bars = viewership increase · Red bars = decrease",
    x = "Episode (by season)",
    y = "Change in viewers (millions)",
    caption = "Source: Wikipedia — List of Breaking Bad episodes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_markdown(size = 15, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey45", size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(colour = "grey60", size = 9)
  )
```

---

## Key Observations

```{r inline-calcs}
s1_avg  <- viewership$Avg_Viewers_M[1]
s2_avg  <- viewership$Avg_Viewers_M[2]
s5_avg  <- viewership$Avg_Viewers_M[5]
s4_avg  <- viewership$Avg_Viewers_M[4]
s1_to_s4_growth <- round(s4_avg - s1_avg, 2)
s4_to_s5_growth <- round(s5_avg - s4_avg, 2)
pct_s1_s5 <- round((s5_avg - s1_avg) / s1_avg * 100, 1)
finale_viewers <- 10.28
s5b_ep9 <- 5.92
jump_to_finale <- round(finale_viewers - s5b_ep9, 2)
```

*Breaking Bad* began its run as a modest cable drama and ended as a cultural phenomenon. Several patterns stand out:

**Slow but steady early growth.** Across Seasons 1 through 4, average viewership climbed steadily from `r s1_avg`M to `r s4_avg`M — an increase of `r s1_to_s4_growth`M viewers per season on average. Growth was consistent but not explosive during this period.

**The Season 5 explosion.** The return of the show for its final eight episodes (Season 5B, from August 2013) triggered a remarkable surge. Average viewership across all of Season 5 reached `r s5_avg`M — a jump of `r s4_to_s5_growth`M over Season 4's average. From Season 1 to Season 5, average viewership grew by `r pct_s1_s5`%.

**The series finale broke records.** The final episode, *"Felina"*, aired on September 29, 2013, and drew `r finale_viewers`M viewers — the largest audience in the show's history and a gain of `r jump_to_finale`M over the Season 5B premiere episode (`r s5b_ep9`M). This made it the most-watched scripted episode in AMC's history at the time.

**Within-season patterns.** Seasons 1–4 followed a mild U-shape: viewership typically dipped slightly mid-season before recovering toward finales. Season 5 defied this pattern entirely, with near-monotonic growth driven by intense audience anticipation for the show's conclusion.

The data tell a story of a slow-burn classic that found its mass audience only in its final stretch — largely fuelled by binge-watching on Netflix, which introduced millions of new viewers to the show just months before the final episodes aired.

---

*Data sourced from Wikipedia: [List of Breaking Bad episodes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Breaking_Bad_episodes).*
